fresh_facts.claim: US military forces conducted extensive airstrikes on over 7,000 Iranian targets, including missile production facilities, air defenses, and naval assets, with the largest strike package announced for April 5, 2026.
US Central Command reported degrading Iran's ballistic missile and drone capabilities by 90% through ongoing military operations.
President Trump issued a 48-hour deadline expiring around April 7, 8 PM ET for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, threatening strikes on power plants, bridges, and civilian infrastructure.
A US airman downed deep inside Iran was rescued alive, according to Trump statement on April 5, 2026.
Iran warned of 'much more devastating' retaliation if civilian targets are hit again, with its military stating next operations would be 'widespread.'
Pakistan proposed a two-phase ceasefire plan to Iran and the US, which Tehran is reviewing but rejecting if it allows renewed war risks.
A US 15-point proposal delivered via Pakistan to Iran includes Strait reopening, missile limits, sanctions relief, and nuclear rollback limits.
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi confirmed review of a US proposal but denied intent to negotiate.
Trump issued a deadline of Tuesday, 8:00 P.M. Eastern Time (approximately April 8, 2026) before threatening to unleash 'hell' on Iran's power grid and infrastructure.
Trump postponed infrastructure strikes for 5 days after 'productive' talks led by envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner with a top Iranian official.
Iran's Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf denied that negotiations with the US occurred.
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was killed on February 28 in US-Israeli strikes.
A Provisional Leadership Council comprising President Masoud Pezeshkian, judiciary chief Gholamhossein Mohseni-Ejei, and cleric Alireza Arafi holds interim power following Khamenei's death.
The Assembly of Experts, hit by an Israeli strike in Qom on March 3 during succession talks, appointed Mojtaba Khamenei as new Supreme Leader.
Iran named hardliner Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr, ex-IRGC deputy, as Supreme National Security Council chief, replacing killed Ali Larijani.
Iranian hardliners labeled ex-Foreign Minister Javad Zarif a 'traitor' for urging war's end and demanded his arrest.
Iranian hardliners demanded the arrest of ex-President Hassan Rouhani for unspecified reasons.
Mojtaba Khamenei issued a statement removing adversaries from security positions.
Iran executed at least five men last week on anti-government and security charges, including a fourth protester for a Basij base fire during January protests.
Iran agreed to release 20 cargo ships starting Monday amid Strait of Hormuz blockade.
OPEC+ hiked oil output and warned of slow recovery following military attacks.
Turkish, Egyptian, and Pakistani representatives mediated between Iran and the US without tangible results.
Israel killed an IRGC intelligence chief in Tehran and struck near Sharif University.
Jordan intercepted Iranian missiles and unmanned aerial vehicles.
Iranian-linked groups hit US sites in Iraq.
The IRGC hid a missile launcher in a civilian tunnel.

regime_fall.current_status: Day 38 of conflict shows escalating military pressure with CENTCOM's 7,000+ strikes degrading Iranian capabilities by 90%, while diplomatic channels through Pakistan, Egypt, and Turkey show contradictory signals—Trump extended a 5-day pause after 'productive' talks despite Iran's public denial of negotiations and hardliner consolidation under new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei and hardliner Zolghadr.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Trump postponed infrastructure strikes for 5 days after 'productive' talks led by envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner with a top Iranian official, while Iran publicly denied negotiations occurred and hardliners demanded arrests of moderate figures including ex-President Rouhani.
